question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch parse path where a HashMismatchException on file N causes onEnd() to never be called for any file in the batch, resulting in all records from files 0 through N-1 being silently dropped from the mirror node export even though they were successfully parsed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile with a manipulated loadStart/loadEnd timestamp pair that causes the totalDurationMetric to record a negative or zero duration, and if metric thresholds gate processing decisions, cause the mirror node to incorrectly skip or misprocess subsequent files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile whose name does not conform to the expected StreamFilename timestamp format, causing onEnd() to persist a file with an unparseable name that breaks downstream filename-based lookups and causes records to be inaccessible via the mirror node REST API?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SqlEntityListener.onEnd() isEnabled() check to cause a scenario where the SQL listener is disabled mid-stream, causing onEnd() to be a no-op and all buffered records in the ParserContext to be discarded without being persisted to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with amendments that replace legitimate RecordStreamItems with crafted ones, causing onEnd() to be called with a RecordFile whose items reflect the attacker's amendments rather than the original consensus records, resulting in incorrect records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a hapiVersion that triggers a migration listener's onEnd() (e.g., SyntheticCryptoTransferApprovalMigration) to run an async migration that modifies previously committed records, causing the mirror node to retroactively export incorrect historical records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the last AtomicReference is only updated after onEnd() returns successfully, such that a concurrent parse() call (before synchronization takes effect) reads a stale last value and incorrectly accepts a file that should have been rejected, causing duplicate or out-of-order records in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile with a null bytes field (already cleared) to onEnd(), causing a NullPointerException or silent failure in a listener that attempts to access the raw bytes for hash verification, resulting in unverified records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a manipulated digestAlgorithm field (e.g., set to UNKNOWN) that is accepted by onEnd() without re-validation, causing the mirror node to export records with an incorrect digest algorithm label and mislead integrity verification tools?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RecordFileParser's doParse() publishing a RecordFileParsedEvent before onEnd() completes, such that an event consumer reads the RecordFile state before it is fully committed by onEnd(), causing the consumer to act on incomplete or incorrect record data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with a very large number of RecordItems that causes the @Transactional timeout to expire during doParse() item processing, resulting in onEnd() never being called and the entire file's records being silently dropped from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a manipulated index (block number) that is identical to an already-committed block, causing onEnd() to persist a duplicate block entry and the mirror node to export two conflicting records for the same block number?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of null-checking on the RecordFile parameter in onEnd() implementations, by triggering a code path that passes a null RecordFile to onEnd(), causing a NullPointerException that aborts the flush and leaves previously parsed records uncommitted in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file with a startObjectRunningHash that does not match the endObjectRunningHash of the previous file, and if the stream type is not chained, bypass the hash chain check in shouldParse() so that onEnd() is called with a file that breaks the integrity chain, causing incorrect records to be exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile with a manipulated softwareVersion (major/minor/patch) that triggers version-specific processing branches in onEnd() implementations, causing incorrect record transformation or migration logic to be applied and exporting wrong records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch parse() method's single doFlush() call at the end, such that if doParse() for any intermediate file throws an unchecked exception, onEnd() is never called for any file in the batch, causing all records from the entire batch to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a consensusEnd value of Long.MAX_VALUE, causing arithmetic overflow in parseLatencyMetric.record(Duration.between(...)) after onEnd() completes, and if this causes an exception, preventing the last.set() from being called and corrupting the parser's chain state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with RecordItems that have non-monotonically-increasing consensusTimestamps, causing the RecordItem.previous chain to be built incorrectly before onEnd() is called, resulting in the mirror node exporting records with incorrect parent-child transaction relationships?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that streamFile.clear() is called unconditionally in doFlush() after onEnd(), such that if onEnd() stores the RecordFile reference for deferred processing, the cleared state causes subsequent reads to return null data and produce missing records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a size field that does not match the actual bytes length, causing onEnd() to persist incorrect size metadata and downstream mirror node consumers to reject or misparse the record file when attempting to re-read it by size?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Retryable annotation on parse() to cause onEnd() to be called multiple times for the same RecordFile when a transient database error occurs, and if the listener implementation is not idempotent, cause duplicate or corrupted records to be exported by the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with a manipulated blockNumber (index) of zero or negative, causing the updateIndex() migration to compute a negative offset and call repository.updateIndex() with a value that shifts all existing block numbers incorrectly, corrupting the block index exported by the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a hapiVersionMajor/Minor/Patch combination that causes a migration listener's onEnd() to incorrectly determine that a migration boundary has been crossed, triggering an unintended async migration that modifies existing records and causes the mirror node to export incorrect historical data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the CompositeRecordStreamFileListener does not catch exceptions from individual listeners' onEnd() calls, such that a RuntimeException from one listener propagates up and prevents subsequent listeners from receiving the onEnd() notification, causing partial record persistence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordStreamFileListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onEnd(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file with a manipulated previousHash that is exactly equal to the current file's hash, creating a self-referential hash chain that passes the contentEquals check in shouldParse() and causes onEnd() to commit a file with a circular hash reference, corrupting the chain integrity exported by the mirror node?
